nextSession.thesis: MORNING (7/14, 11:00 ET) — the month's densest binary RESOLVED BULLISHLY. June CPI printed cool/in-line AND the heavy Q2 big-bank open (JPM/WFC/C/BAC/GS) BEAT pre-open, so the tape is in a confirmed relief reclaim: SPY 751.97 (+1.43% over its 50-day, −0.74% off the all-time high), QQQ 719.68 RECLAIMED its 50-day (+0.42%, mom5 flipped +1.44), VIX 16.51 (−0.65 on the day, low/calm, positive-gamma/orderly). XLF took the CLEAN Leading quadrant on the bank beats (rsRatio 101.63); XLE is the best-accelerating sector (accel +1.67); semis bounced hard on the relief (NVDA/AVGO/AMD all green). The left tail that the pre-market runs hedged is gone for now — BUT the 7/29 FOMC hike tail WIDENED to ~46.5% (a near coin-flip vs ~15-18% a week ago), the underpriced two-week asymmetry. Posture for the rest of 7/14 into 7/29: HOLD the reclaim, ADD the confirmed XLF leadership on an orderly pullback, ROTATE XLE over rolling XLI, TRIM the stretched semis extension INTO the relief spike, and cut the CPI hedge to a defined-risk STUB carried into the FOMC. Don't chase the spike at the highs — the next binary is the Fed, not the print just passed.

actions.subject: SPY / QQQ / SPMO / VGT core longs
actions.rationale: Cool CPI + broad bank beats re-confirmed the bull and QQQ RECLAIMED its 50-day (+0.42%, mom5 flipped to +1.44); SPMO retook the #1 6M momentum rank (27.12%). The core is bull-stacked with strong medium-term momentum (SPY mom126/252 +8.92%/+21.92%; QQQ mom63 +16.7, mom252 +30.48). Hold and let it carry — the reclaim is confirmation, not a new entry.
actions.structuralAnchor: SPY 20>50>200; 50-day ~741.4 (spot +1.43%), 200-day ~692 (+8.66%), −0.74% off the 52-week high. QQQ reclaimed its 50-day (~716.7, +0.42%). SPMO +20.18% / VGT +17.67% over their 200-days.
actions.trigger: Hold while SPY holds the rising 50-day (~741) and QQQ holds its reclaimed 50-day (~717); add only on a pullback that holds support, NOT into the relief spike at the highs.
actions.invalidation: A confirmed daily close below the SPY 50-day (~741) or QQQ losing its 50-day again (~717), or a broad loss of the QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacks.

actions.subject: XLF (financials) — confirmed clean Leading on the bank beats
actions.rationale: The Q2 money-center beats (GS, JPM, upbeat NII) put XLF into the CLEAN Leading quadrant (rsRatio 101.63, rsMom 101.11, accel +0.90, bull-stack +8.22% over its 200-day) — the pre-market Peaking ⚠ fear was rejected. A tool-aligned add with a live, positive earnings catalyst and the only sector in Leading.
actions.structuralAnchor: XLF Leading quadrant, rsRatio 101.63 (>100), rsMom 101.11, accel +0.90, bull-stack (+8.22% over its 200-day).
actions.trigger: Add on an orderly pullback that holds the breakout while XLF stays in Leading (rsRatio >100) above its rising 50-day; do NOT chase the earnings-gap high.
actions.invalidation: XLF loses the Leading quadrant (rsRatio back <100) or a failed close below its rising 50-day — the bank-beat leadership fades.

actions.subject: Add XLE vs trim rolling XLI
actions.rationale: The tool-aligned 2-week RRG accel keeps energy the freshest rotation INTO even post-CPI: XLE Improving ↑ (accel +1.67, the board's best; rsMom6m +13.85; +10.68% over its 200-day) one cross from Leading, while XLI is Weakening ↓ (accel −1.94, the sharpest roll of a bull-stacked sector). The confirmed cyclical hand-off.
actions.structuralAnchor: XLE Improving quadrant, accel +1.67 (best on the board), rsRatio 99.5 (one cross from Leading); XLI Weakening ↓, accel −1.94.
actions.trigger: Add XLE on its rsRatio crossing 100 into Leading; fund by trimming XLI on any failure of its rising 50-day.
actions.invalidation: XLE acceleration rolls back negative or oil (USO 30, Mixed, mom21 −7.49) breaks lower on a CPI-energy air-pocket, aborting the energy turn.

actions.subject: Stretched semis (SOXX) into the relief strength
actions.rationale: SOXX is the strongest structure on the board (+47.12% over its 200-day) but Weakening ↓ on the 2-week accel (rsMom 99.13 <100) and stretched far above trend, now bouncing HARD on the relief (NVDA +5.44 / AVGO +6.7 / AMD +7.74) with MRVL mom21 −20.59 the internal tell. Trim the extension INTO the spike; keep the core — de-risking a stretched leader, not selling the theme.
actions.structuralAnchor: SOXX +47.12% over its 200-day, Weakening ↓ (rsMom 99.13 <100), 8/12 bull-stacked; MRVL mom21 −20.59 the tell.
actions.trigger: Trim into the relief strength / on SOXX losing its rising 20-day; re-add only when rsMom crosses back above 100 with breadth improving.
actions.invalidation: SOXX reclaims a rising RS-momentum (>100) and turns rotation-into — the leadership re-accelerates and the trim was early.

actions.subject: Cut the CPI hedge to a defined-risk STUB carried into the 7/29 FOMC
actions.rationale: The CPI binary resolved cool so the acute left tail is spent — take the CPI hedge OFF and bank the decay, but the 7/29 FOMC hike tail WIDENED to ~46.5% (CME FedWatch, near coin-flip) into a short-vol tape at the highs with VIX 16.51 (cheap). Keep a small defined-risk STUB (put-spread or VIX-call) sized to the Fed gap, not a standing short — this is the underpriced two-week asymmetry.
actions.structuralAnchor: VIX 16.51 (low, −0.65) with SPY −0.74% off the ATH — spot near the highs + cheap vol = maximum asymmetry to carry a small hedge into a live ~46.5% hike tail.
actions.trigger: Close the CPI hedge into the relief; roll a small defined-risk put-spread / VIX-call stub to the 7/29 FOMC. Size to the Fed gap, don't over-hedge a bull tape.
actions.invalidation: The 7/29 hike tail collapses (a dovish Fed-speaker path / cooling PPI+retail narrows FedWatch back toward ~15-20% hold-dominant) — let the stub decay; or VIX pushes >19-20 and the stub is already working.

dataAsOf.macro: VIX 16.51 (Yahoo ^VIX, LIVE 7/14) — LOW / calm, DOWN −0.65 from the 17.16 pre-CPI carry, BELOW the 3-pt regime-change threshold, so the regime is positive-gamma / orderly and the cool print RELIEVED vol. dataAsOf.events: Near-term calendar: June CPI Tue 7/14 8:30am ET (BLS) is now PAST and printed COOL/in-line (web-verified 7/14); the heavy Q2 big-bank open the SAME morning — JPMorgan + Wells Fargo + Bank of America + Citigroup + Goldman Sachs, all pre-open — BEAT (XLF into clean Leading). FORWARD, nearest-first: June PPI 7/15 8:30am + Morgan Stanley pre-open (the last big-bank print); June retail sales + weekly initial jobless claims 7/16 8:30am; monthly OPEX 7/17; FOMC 7/29 2:00pm (non-SEP, no new dots) collides with the MSFT FY26 Q4 print AMC 7/29; July NFP 8/7. FOMC path (WEB-RE-VERIFIED 7/14 post-CPI, CME FedWatch): the cool CPI did NOT collapse the tail — ~53.5% HOLD / ~46.5% HIKE, funds 3.50-3.75% held four straight meetings, no 2026 cuts, hawkish; the ~46.5% hike tail is the underpriced two-week asymmetry. Consensus print figures for 7/15-16 remain ESTIMATES — RE-VERIFY each at release.

breadth.note: STRUCTURALLY BROAD and leadership CONFIRMED a clean Leading sector this bar. Tool-aligned RRG (2-week RS accel, live 7/14): XLF is now the CLEAN Leading sector (accel +0.90, rsRatio 101.63, bull-stack +8.22% >200d) on the bank beats — the pre-market Peaking ⚠ flip was rejected. Rotation INTO also for XLE (Improving ↑, accel +1.67, the board's best; +10.68% >200d), XLC (Improving ↑, accel +0.54, bear-stacked) and XLB (Improving ↑, accel +0.37). Rotation OUT / Weakening ↓: XLK (accel −0.15, but still the top structural RS at +19.95% >200d), XLV (−1.76), XLI (−1.94, the sharpest roll of a bull-stacked sector). MAGS complex Improving ↑ (accel +0.74, rsMom 100.87). Lagging/neutral: XLY, XLP. Proxy from sector-member bars, not an exchange breadth feed. Author rotation from RRG state/accel, NOT the raw 1-month RS level.

regime.note: STRUCTURE FIRST, on the LIVE 7/14 bar with the month's densest binary RESOLVED BULLISHLY: a healthy tech-led bull near all-time highs that just repaired its one near-term crack. SPY 751.97 bull-stacked (20>50>200), −0.74% off its 52-week high, +8.66% over a rising 200-day, mom126/252 +8.92%/+21.92%. 4th distinct bar of the theme (7/09→7/10→7/13→7/14 — moderate-to-high persistence, a NEW distinct 7/14 observation that EXTENDED the bull); the cool June CPI + broad Q2 bank beats drove a relief reclaim — QQQ RECLAIMED its 50-day (+0.42%, mom5 flipped +1.44), VIX fell to 16.51 (low/calm, −0.65). Three features on the confirmed bull: (1) leadership CONFIRMED — XLF took the clean Leading quadrant on the bank beats (rsRatio 101.63), XLE the best accel (+1.67) one cross from Leading, MAGS Improving ↑; the only caution is XLK/SOXX Weakening ↓ on the 2-week accel despite the board's best structural RS (semis stretched +47.12% >200d and bouncing hard on the relief); (2) the lone STRUCTURAL crack, MSFT (bear-stack, −28.06% off its high, mom5 −0.35) FAILED to join the relief — a bearish relative tell into its 7/29 capex guide; (3) NO haven bid even on a risk-on tape — GLD 16.1 / SLV 0 / BTC 41.5 all Downtrend (scores ticked up but no trend turn), only agriculture (DBA 54.3) firm. All under a HAWKISH-HOLD Fed whose 7/29 HIKE tail WIDENED to ~46.5% (the underpriced two-week asymmetry). Posture: HOLD the reclaim; ADD confirmed XLF; ROTATE XLE over rolling XLI; TRIM stretched semis INTO strength; cut the CPI hedge to a defined-risk STUB into the 7/29 FOMC; stay OUT of havens; do NOT call an MSFT bottom until it reclaims ~402.

SPY 751.97 bull-stacked (20>50>200), −0.74% off the 52-week high, +8.66% over a rising 200-day; SPMO/VGT bull-stacked, QQQ RECLAIMED its 50-day (+0.42%, mom5 flipped +1.44). Leadership CONFIRMED under an intact index: XLF took the clean Leading quadrant on the bank beats, XLE the best accel, MAGS Improving ↑ — the only caution is tech/semis Weakening ↓ on the 2-week accel while stretched. The single structural exception is MSFT (bear-stack, −28.06% off high) which FAILED to join the relief.

Sector breadth broad — 8/11 bull-stacked and above their 200-days, XLF now the clean Leading sector. SOXX 8/12 bull-stacked, +47.12% over the 200-day (extended, Weakening ↓). backdrop.macroCycle: HAWKISH-HOLD Fed: funds 3.50-3.75% held four straight meetings, no 2026 cuts priced, hawkish. 7/29 FOMC (non-SEP) modal HOLD (~53.5%) with a LIVE ~46.5% HIKE tail (WEB-RE-VERIFIED 7/14 post-CPI, CME FedWatch) — the cool June CPI did NOT collapse the tail, it stayed a near coin-flip and is the underpriced two-week asymmetry the short-vol tape (VIX 16.51) is ignoring. WTI carried/STALE ~$71-72; USO Mixed with mom21 −7.49 — soft crude eased the CPI headline tail; a Strait-of-Hormuz re-escalation is the residual energy/inflation risk into the Fed. Re-verify the priced path + WTI live.
globalBackdrop: GLOBAL ROTATION is Asia-led and confirms the onshore semis / AI-hardware supply chain: EWS (Singapore) leads at 74.3/100 vs ACWI (rel21 +9.6, Strong uptrend), EWT (Taiwan) 71.6 next (rel63 +22.43, rel126 +45.6), then EWJ 63.7, EWC 60.9, EWY 57.7, EWU 54.5 — but EVERY leader carries an FX headwind / confirms-weakness (EWS fxStrength −1.53, EWJ −2.01, EWC −2.37), so the equity strength is local-currency, not a weak-dollar tailwind (deep-link: global-rotation-lab.html).

AI-CAPEX CYCLE is the dominant secular driver — MSFT's structural breakdown (bear-stack, −28.06% off its high, mom5 −0.35 failing to join the relief) vs the AI-hardware/semis bull-stacks (VGT/SPMO near highs, SOXX +47.12% over its 200-day and bouncing hard on the relief, AMD/MU on triple-digit 63-day momentum) IS the "show-me" (software) vs "build-it" (hardware/semis) dispersion at the single-name level. The MSFT capex guide 7/29 is the tell (deep-link: ai-capex-strategy-lab.html).

REAL ASSETS show NO haven bid even on the risk-on relief tape: GLD Downtrend (16.1, mom63 −13.99), SLV washed out (0, mom63 −21.68), BTC/IBIT Downtrend (41.5 / 35.2) — scores TICKED UP with the risk-on move but none turned trend. Agriculture (DBA 54.3) is the only firm real-asset trend; broad commodities (DBC 43.9) and oil (USO 30) are Mixed. Net: risk-on, disinflationary-at-the-margin — leadership is equities (confirmed financials + the bull), not havens (deep-link: real-assets-lab.html).
backdrop.pricedIn: A cool-to-in-line CPI and orderly bank beats WERE the base case the short-vol tape (VIX 16.51, low) was positioned for, and they delivered — the relief reclaim is largely the crowd being paid on that base case. What is NOT priced: the 7/29 FOMC ~46.5% hike tail (a near coin-flip the calm tape is ignoring), a sticky 7/15 PPI that keeps the tail live, and whether MSFT's failure to bounce foreshadows a soft 7/29 capex guide.
backdrop.asymmetry: Skewed to the downside over the next two weeks despite the bullish print: spot is at the highs (SPY −0.74% off the ATH) with VIX 16.51 (low/cheap) and a LIVE ~46.5% 7/29 hike tail — so a hawkish Fed surprise has far more room to move price than another leg of relief (already largely realized). The cheap-vol + near-high combination is the setup to carry a small defined-risk hedge into the Fed, not to add fresh length.
backdrop.whatWouldChangeIt: A confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~741) or QQQ losing its reclaimed 50-day again (~717); VIX (16.51) pushing >19-20 or the term structure inverting; a sticky 7/15 PPI / 7/16 retail sales that pushes the 7/29 hike tail decisively >50%; or MSFT reclaiming its 50-day (~402) to end the lone structural downtrend. The 7/29 FOMC (with its widened hike tail) is now the single fastest regime-changer — the CPI binary that dominated the pre-market is past.

recommendations.rationale: XLE best-accelerating on the board (accel +1.67, rsMom6m +13.85); XLI rolling out (accel −1.94). The confirmed cyclical rotation, one cross from Leading.

recommendations.structuralAnchor: SOXX +47.12% over its 200-day, Weakening ↓ (rsMom 99.13 <100), 8/12 bull-stacked; MRVL mom21 −20.59 the tell.
recommendations.trigger: Trim into the relief strength / on SOXX losing its rising 20-day; re-add when rsMom crosses back above 100 with breadth improving.
recommendations.invalidation: SOXX reclaims a rising RS-momentum (>100) and turns rotation-into — the leadership re-accelerates and the trim was early.
recommendations.rationale: Strongest structure but Weakening ↓/out on the 2-week accel and the most extended on the board; bouncing hard on the relief (NVDA +5.44/AVGO +6.7/AMD +7.74) — trim the extension into strength.

recommendations.structuralAnchor: GLD mom63 −13.99, SLV mom63 −21.68 (washed out), BTC-USD score 41.5 Downtrend — a risk-on / disinflationary-at-the-margin real-asset tape, not a fear tape.
recommendations.trigger: Stay OUT; re-engage metals only if GLD reclaims an uptrend AND SLV lifts off a 0 score; BTC only above its downtrend.
recommendations.invalidation: A Hormuz-driven energy re-spike revives a gold/inflation-hedge bid (GLD reclaims trend) — then the no-haven read is stale and gold becomes the tell into 7/29.
recommendations.rationale: No haven bid on the relief tape — all three Downtrend; leadership is equities (financials + the confirmed bull), not havens.

events.consensus: ESTIMATE (re-verify at release) — 8:30 ET PPI (PCE feed-through); MS reports pre-open as the bank tape continues after the 7/14 beats

scenarios.name: confirms-cool
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bullish — PPI confirms the cool 7/14 CPI, PCE read-through benign, some of the ~46.5% hike tail eases

scenarios.name: inline
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: muted — PPI in line, the relief rally holds; MS extends or fades the bank-beat leadership

scenarios.name: confirms-hot
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bearish-at-margin — sticky pipeline PPI keeps the widened hike tail live despite the cool CPI (the Hormuz-energy path)

events.psychologyNote: The FIRST forward confirm/deny to the cool CPI. A confirming-cool PPI narrows the hike tail the relief tape is watching; a sticky PPI is the tell that the ~46.5% 7/29 hike is real. MS is the last of the big-bank tape — watch whether it extends the XLF Leading read.

scenarios.name: soft-landing

scenarios.name: weak
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: mixed — soft retail / rising claims stokes growth worry but eases the widened hike tail

scenarios.name: hot

events.consensus: Standard monthly expiry — lands 3 days after the cool-CPI relief; with the binary resolved benign, positive dealer gamma should re-lengthen and pin the range unless PPI/retail surprise

scenarios.name: pin

scenarios.name: unwind-up

scenarios.name: unwind-down

events.psychologyNote: The gamma release valve on the data week. With the CPI binary resolved benign and VIX at 16.51, the base case is a positive-gamma pin — but re-pull the chain live before trusting any wall; the Tier-A slice has no wall levels.

events.event: FOMC decision + MSFT FY26 Q4 print
events.consensus: WEB-VERIFIED 7/14 (CME FedWatch, post-CPI) — 2:00 ET decision (non-SEP, no new dots), ~53.5% hold / ~46.5% HIKE / ~1% cut; MSFT reports AMC (capex guide is the AI tell)

scenarios.name: dovish-hold

events.psychologyNote: The dominant month-end binary and the single biggest asymmetry from here: the hike tail WIDENED to ~46.5% even on the cool CPI (Hormuz energy risk + core above target), and it lands the SAME day as the MSFT capex guide — stacking the macro shock with the AI "show-me vs build-it" tell. This is the two-week risk the relief tape is underpricing.

psychology.read: NAMED REGIME: a calm, positive-gamma, tech-led bull that just RESOLVED its densest binary of the month BULLISHLY — the June CPI printed cool (headline −0.4% MoM / core 0.0% MoM) and the big banks beat broadly (GS +8%, JPM +2%+), VIX fell to 16.51, QQQ reclaimed its 50-day, and XLF re-took a clean Leading quadrant. The crowd's posture is RELIEVED-AND-RE-ENGAGING — the fat left tail (hot CPI / bank miss) is gone, so the tape lifts near the highs and dealer gamma should re-lengthen positive. NOTE the deterministic composite score of 0 is VIX-only (Fear&Greed was NOT fetched this run), NOT a measured neutral and NOT a VIX data gap — VIX is present and low (16.51). The subtle danger is COMPLACENCY: the crowd is treating a cool CPI as an all-clear on the Fed, but the 7/29 hike tail WIDENED to ~46.5% (CME FedWatch, post-CPI) — cool consumer prices did NOT tame the path because a Strait-of-Hormuz energy re-spike and above-target core keep a live hike on the table. That is the underpriced two-week asymmetry. What would FALSIFY the read: a VIX push back >19-20 or term-structure inversion (regime flip), a confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~741) or QQQ losing its just-reclaimed 50-day (~717) (relief-rally failure), the market pricing/ delivering the ~46.5% 7/29 hike (the biggest single falsifier from here), or MSFT reclaiming its 50-day (~402) and MAGS breadth broadening (a bullish broadening that ends the lone structural crack).
inputs: 4th DISTINCT daily bar of the theme (7/09→7/10→7/13→7/14); this is a NEW distinct 7/14 intraday observation that EXTENDED the bull post-CPI — moderate-to-high persistence. QQQ 719.68 RECLAIMED its 50-day (+0.42%, mom5 +1.44) on this bar

June CPI WEB-VERIFIED cool (headline −0.4% MoM / +3.5% YoY; core 0.0% MoM / +2.6% YoY — biggest monthly drop since Apr-2020, −9.7% gasoline); Q2 big banks WEB-VERIFIED beat (GS +8%, JPM +2%+, C/WFC upbeat NII)

RRG rotation CLARIFIED: XLF now clean Leading (accel +0.90, rsRatio 101.63, bank beats confirm); XLE Improving/into (accel +1.67, board best); XLK/SOXX Weakening/out; MAGS Improving on thin breadth (accel +0.74, 1/7 bull-stacked)

VIX 16.51 (low/calm, LIVE, −0.65 from the 17.16 pre-CPI carry); Fear&Greed NOT fetched → composite is VIX-only (score 0 = VIX-only, NOT a data gap). Term structure not in the Tier-A slice (re-pull live)
Real assets: no haven bid on the relief tape — GLD 16.1 / SLV 0 / BTC 41.5 all Downtrend; DBA 54.3 the only firm trend (risk-on, disinflationary-at-the-margin)
Forward catalysts: 7/15 PPI+MS, 7/16 retail+claims, 7/17 OPEX, 7/29 FOMC+MSFT — the 7/29 hike tail WIDENED to ~46.5% (CME FedWatch, post-CPI) despite the cool print
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: The base case is now a bullish slow-grind near the highs: the binary resolved benign, the left tail is gone, and the leadership CLARIFIED to a clean cyclical read (XLF Leading, XLE accelerating, QQQ back above its 50-day). But with much of the relief already priced −0.74% off the ATH, chasing the spike has limited upside — the cleaner edge is PRESSING the confirmed rotation (add XLF, rotate XLI→XLE) and TRIMMING the stretched semis into the bounce, not adding index beta at the highs. The one probability tilt AGAINST the crowd: the ~46.5% 7/29 hike tail is underpriced by a relief tape treating cool CPI as an all-clear — carry that as the two-week asymmetry (watch energy / gold as the tell), not a today-hedge. Re-fetch VIX + term structure and the live gamma walls before sizing anything off the vol/positioning read.

Leadership is narrow and top-heavy: AAPL near its 52-week high (the only bull-stack) and META's +16.57 mom21 / +7.54 mom5 surge carry the complex, NVDA is bouncing (+5.44 mom5, tangled at its 50-day), while MSFT (the crack, −28.06% off high, mom5 −0.35 softening on a rally day) and TSLA (bear-stack, mom5 −0.95) lag. An EARLY-accumulation signal under a weak-breadth surface — WATCH for broadening (>1/7 bull-stacked) and an MSFT 50-day reclaim (~402) to confirm; the cool-CPI relief lifted the complex but did NOT broaden it. Author from the RRG state/accel, not the raw 1-month RS.

Semis are bouncing hard on the cool-CPI relief (NVDA +5.44 / AVGO +6.7 / AMD +7.74 / AMAT +6.99 / LRCX +5.52 / KLAC +6.35 on the 5-day) but the extension is the risk: AMD/MU/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC are all far above trend while MRVL (mom21 −20.59, −29.55% off its high) is the profit-taking tell under the surface. TRIM the extension into the relief strength / on a lost 20-day; re-add when rsMom crosses back above 100 with breadth improving — de-risking a stretched leader bouncing into a still-negative accel, not selling the AI-hardware theme. Author from the RRG state/accel, not the raw 1-month RS.

QQQ.status: $720.55 — BULL-STACK (20>50>200) and HELD its 50-day into the close, now +0.54% ABOVE (~716.7) after the completed 7/13 close BELOW it — the structural hold-or-fail line held through the session, the cool CPI + bank beats bought and KEPT the reclaim the prior runs were watching for. +12.97% over the 200-day (~637.7), −3.33% off its 52-week high (~745.4); mom63 +16.84, mom252 +30.64, mom5 +1.57 (positive on the relief). The 2-week tech accel is still soft (XLK Weakening ↓) so this is a reclaim that held, not a fresh breakout — HOLD, the defined-risk hedge stub can carry overnight through 7/15 PPI into the 7/29 FOMC; a close back below ~716.7 re-opens the caution.

SPMO.status: $152.70 — BULL-STACK, +2.24% over the 50-day, +20.35% over the 200-day; mom63 +22.75, mom252 +37.28, mom5 +2.19 (led the relief bounce, held into the close). The strongest structural trend in the watchlist and the #1 6M balanced-momentum ETF (27.31%). Core long; hold, add on a clean higher-high, the held reclaim confirms the momentum-factor core.

VGT.status: $117.18 — BULL-STACK, +1.32% over the 50-day, +17.98% over the 200-day; mom63 +23.77, mom252 +40.46, mom5 +2.73 (strongest 5-day pop in the watchlist). A top-3 risk-adjusted momentum ETF. Pure-tech core long; hold, the bull-stack + RS strengthened alongside the QQQ 50-day hold.

MSFT.status: $385.38 — BEAR-STACK, −4.19% below its 50-day (~402.3), −12.32% below its 200-day (~439.5), −28.46% off its 52-week high (~538.7); mom5 −0.89 (DETERIORATED further into the close — did NOT join the relief and rolled deeper negative), mom21 −1.27, mom63 +0.48. The lone structural crack that FAILED to bounce with the tape while QQQ held its 50-day and VGT/SPMO extended — a bearish relative tell that WIDENED into the close. NOT a trend change either way until it reclaims the 50-day (~402); the 7/29 capex guide is the binary for the AI "show-me vs build-it" dispersion.

experimental.title: Pre-catalyst de-risk detector (intraday-fade + VIX-uptick + leader-flip) — binary-resolved post-mortem
experimental.note: Method: on the session BEFORE a Tier-1 macro binary, score a composite "de-risk tell" = sign(index close drift) + sign(VIX day-change) + count of sectors rolling out (Leading/Improving→Weakening/Peaking) on the fresh bar, then event-study the forward gap conditioned on it. POST-MORTEM this run: the 7/13-into-7/14-CPI setup fired a MODERATE de-risk tell (QQQ closed −0.54% and lost its 50-day, mom5 −1.53; XLI Weakening ↓ accel −2.40 rolling out) — the crowd DID pre-hedge — and the binary then resolved BULLISHLY (CPI cool, banks beat), so QQQ gapped UP and RECLAIMED the 50-day and HELD it into the close (+0.54%, mom5 +1.57). That is the "crowd already hedged → smaller/positive adverse gap" branch of the hypothesis: a pre-print de-risk into a benign print produced a relief reclaim, not a breakdown. One clean observation for the event-study; the VIX day-change SIGN leg still needs a persisted prior-day close (level present, delta not) to be fully computable. Inputs: open→close index drift, VIX day-change (persist prior close), per-sector RRG state transitions, the dated catalyst + its realized outcome. No owning tool captures the pre-catalyst de-risk state-transition timing today.

experimental.title: Rotation-flip detector (Leading→Peaking) — binary-resolved post-mortem
experimental.note: Method: flag any sector crossing out of a strong quad (Leading→Peaking / Improving→Weakening) with negative acceleration on a fresh bar, then event-study the forward 5/10-day sector return conditioned on whether the coincident catalyst confirmed or denied the roll. POST-MORTEM this run: the 7/13 bar flagged the XLI→XLE hand-off (XLI Weakening ↓ rolling out while XLE Improves ↑ rotating in). The coincident 7/14 money-center bank open then BEAT and put XLF into clean Leading — the financials/industrials cyclical read CONFIRMED (cyclical risk-on), and on the live 7/14 pre-close bar the hand-off EXTENDED: XLE accel firmed to +1.84 (rsMom 101.83) while XLI stayed Weakening ↓ (accel −1.98). So the flip was denied for financials (bullish confirmation) but confirmed for the energy-over-industrials rotation — the detector correctly separated the two cyclicals. Inputs: per-sector rsRatio/rsMom/accel history + the sector's next scheduled catalyst + its realized outcome. No owning tool captures the state-transition timing today.
